USA.title: Independent autopsy 'inconclusive' on cause of death for 18-year-old Nolan Wells in Mississippi
USA.summary: An independent autopsy of the body of Nolan Wells shows that the cause of death is undetermined pending an investigation, according to attorney Ben Crump.

USA.title: Greetings from Jerusalem, whose holy sites host some of the oldest colonies of nesting swifts
USA.summary: The scythe-winged birds have nested in the cracks of the Western Wall and other holy sites in Jerusalem for thousands of years.

USA.title: Trump to attend dignified transfer of fallen soldiers. And, Hegseth testifies on Iran
USA.summary: Trump will attend the dignified transfer of U.S. service members killed in the Middle East. And, Pete Hegseth is requesting billions from Congress to help with the rising cost of the war in Iran.

USA.title: Her son was killed by ICE at a traffic stop. She says she's still waiting for justice
USA.summary: Ruben Ray Martinez, 23, was fatally shot by an ICE agent two months into President Trump's second term. His mother says her family has yet to receive justice.

USA.title: U.S. and Iranian attacks continue as tensions mount on a key waterway in the Red Sea
USA.summary: The fighting comes as the Trump administration is seeking billions of dollars more from Congress for the war with Iran, which Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says has already cost $37.5 billion.

USA.title: What forecasters expect this hurricane season, as Tropical Storm Bertha edges along
USA.summary: The El Niño weather pattern could limit how many Atlantic storms form this year. But unusually warm waters temperatures mean the risk of destructive storms is still high.

International.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
International.summary: Karen Carter was found with stab wounds in the Dordogne village she had lived in for over a decade.

International.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
International.summary: Donald Trump says the US will respond any time Iranian forces shoot at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

International.title: Water cannon deployed at India education system protests
International.summary: Indian police deployed water cannons in Raipur at student protests demanding the resignation of the education minister.

International.title: Key takeaways from Khalil al-Hayya’s first speech as Hamas leader
International.summary: The newly appointed leader says Hamas would work to help the people of Gaza and achieve a united Palestinian state.

International.title: Death toll from blast at Indian hydroelectric project rises to 20 people
International.summary: Rescuers say flooding and toxic gas in the collapsed tunnel make it unlikely any remaining trapped workers survived.

International.title: Why the US may stop collecting workplace race and gender data
International.summary: A newly proposed change would eliminate annual workplace demographic reporting used to monitor discrimination trends.

International.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
International.summary: Daniel Siad, whose name appears in the Epstein files thousands of times, denied knowledge of the threat Jeffrey Epstein posed.

International.title: US judge sets 2027 trial date for Venezuela’s Maduro and wife
International.summary: Former President Maduro pleads not guilty to drug charges; lawyer plans to challenge legality of US military operation.

International.title: Mamdani backs off pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
International.summary: The New York City mayor instead called on US authorities to act on International Criminal Court's arrest warrant against the Israeli prime minister.

International.title: Anonymous food donations reach Indian student protesters
International.summary: Anonymous donors are using food delivery apps to send food to student protesters in New Delhi

Sports.title: Menzies withdraws from World Matchplay Darts after becoming unwell on stage
Sports.summary: Cameron Menzies' match against Ross Smith at the World Matchplay Darts was abandoned after the Scot became dizzy on stage and later fainted at the Winter Gardens on Wednesday night.

Sports.title: Aston Villa agree deal to sign Garnacho
Sports.summary: Aston Villa have agreed a deal to sign Alejandro Garnacho from Chelsea.

Sports.title: 'Jofra didn't bother' - Archer accepts his fate after run-out mistake
Sports.summary: In their opening match of the 2026 men's Hundred, Southern Brave's Jofra Archer acknowledges he's going to be run out and accepts his fate by walking off, rather than attempting to make it back to the crease.

Sports.title: Rangers to appeal McInnes four-match touchline ban
Sports.summary: Derek McInnes has been banned from the touchline for his first three domestic games as Rangers manager for his criticism of match officials.

Sports.title: Villa agree loan deal for Chelsea winger Garnacho
Sports.summary: Aston Villa agree a loan deal for Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho until the end of the 2026-27 season, with an obligation to buy.

Sports.title: How close is that?! Kellaway's unbelievable catch on the boundary
Sports.summary: Ben Kellaway makes a brilliant catch and then manages to fall just inside the boundary rope to remove Tristan Stubbs as the Southern Brave fall to 70-4 in their opening match against Welsh Fire in their opening match of the men's 2026 Hundred.

Sports.title: World Matchplay Darts LIVE! Van Veen wins battle vs Nijman after Menzies withdraws through illness
Rangers to fight McInnes ban for 'disgusting' penalty remark
Sports.summary: Rangers intend to contest a four-match touchline ban given to manager Derek McInnes for describing as "disgusting" the decision to award Celtic a late penalty at Motherwell in May.

Sports.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
Sports.summary: Major League Soccer says it is investigating allegations of tampering by Inter Miami after the club signed Brazil midfielder Casemiro.

Sports.title: Welsh Fire collapse as Southern Brave snatch victory
Sports.summary: Welsh Fire lose five wickets for six runs as they collapse from 109-3 to 118-8 and fall to an eight-run defeat against Southern Brave in their opening match of the women's Hundred.

World Politics.title: Hungary: Ex-PM Orban pledges resistance to Magyar 'tyranny'
World Politics.summary: Hungary's former prime minister alleges that his successor has "dismantled the rule of law and eliminated checks and balances." Peter Magyar has pursued Viktor Orban and his allies since winning April's elections.

AI-Buzz.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
AI-Buzz.summary: OpenAI made a mistake setting up what it called a “highly isolated” testing environment and sandbox. According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Hugging Face possible.

AI-Buzz.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
AI-Buzz.summary: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

AI-Buzz.title: Hyundai claims humanoid robot plan is not part of talks with striking workers
AI-Buzz.summary: Union previously warned automaker that any robot deployment must be negotiated.

AI-Buzz.title: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads
AI-Buzz.summary: Yope, a fast-growing social app focused on private groups of friends and family, has raised $12.3 million in seed funding. Instead of chasing creators and algorithmic feeds, the startup is betting that the future of social networking lies in small, private communities powered by…

AI-Buzz.title: Monday.com lays off hundreds to focus on AI
AI-Buzz.summary: The company said it is reducing its headcount by 20%, or about 630 staff, to "support a leaner, more focused operating model" as it focuses on its AI Work Platform.

AI-Buzz.summary: "This is day one for cybersecurity in the age of agents," Hugging Face CEO says.

AI-Buzz.title: Arcee, a US open source AI lab, says Chinese models are not inherently dangerous
AI-Buzz.summary: As Chinese AI models grow in capability and popularity among U.S. companies, the arguing over what should be done about them has reached a fever pitch.

AI-Buzz.title: Substack’s new tool tells you who’s been writing their newsletters with AI
AI-Buzz.summary: Substack is giving readers a way to estimate how much of a newsletter was written by AI, signaling a broader shift toward transparency around AI-assisted content.

AI-Buzz.title: OpenAI’s AI spending spree has ballooned to $750B
AI-Buzz.summary: OpenAI will spend the equivalent of Sweden's GDP on infrastructure through 2030.
